tool.name: LC-MS Peak Picker
tool.description: XCMS CentWave peak detection from raw LC-MS data (.mzML or .mzXML)

param.name: input_file
param.label: Raw LC-MS file
param.help: Upload a single .mzML or .mzXML file. Use Galaxy collections to process multiple files in batch.

param.name: ppm
param.label: PPM error margin
param.help: Mass accuracy tolerance in parts per million. Default: 15

param.name: sn
param.label: Signal-to-noise threshold
param.help: Minimum signal-to-noise ratio for peak detection. Default: 1.5

param.name: max_pw
param.label: Maximum peakwidth (seconds)
param.help: Longest expected peak. Increase to 60+ for lipids. Default: 20

param.name: pref_k
param.label: Prefilter: k (minimum scans)
param.help: Minimum number of scans above intensity threshold. Default: 0

param.name: pref_i
param.label: Prefilter: I (minimum intensity)
param.help: Minimum intensity threshold for prefilter. Default: 0

tool.help:
**LC-MS Peak Picker** ===================== Identifies mass spectrometry peaks from raw LC-MS data using the XCMS CentWave algorithm.
**Inputs** ---------- - A single **.mzML** or **.mzXML** file from LC-MS acquisition. - To process multiple files, create a **Dataset Collection** in Galaxy and map this tool over it.
**Output** ---------- A tab-separated **.peaks** file with one row per detected peak, containing:
======== ================================================================ Column Description ======== ================================================================ mz Detected m/z value mzmin Minimum m/z of the peak mzmax Maximum m/z of the peak rt Retention time (seconds) at peak apex rtmin Retention time at peak start rtmax Retention time at peak end into Integrated peak intensity intb Baseline-corrected integrated intensity maxo Maximum peak intensity sn Signal-to-noise ratio sample Sample name (from input filename) ======== ================================================================
**Parameters** -------------- - **PPM error margin**: Mass accuracy of your instrument. Typical values: 5–15 ppm for Orbitrap, 15–25 ppm for Q-TOF. - **S/N threshold**: Lower values detect more peaks but increase false positives. - **Peakwidth**: Set based on your chromatographic method. Increase max for lipid analyses. - **Prefilter**: Leave at 0,0 for maximum sensitivity.
**Batch processing** -------------------- To process an entire experiment: 1. Upload all .mzML/.mzXML files to Galaxy 2. Select them all → **Build Dataset Collection** 3. Run this tool with **Apply to Collection** 4. All .peaks files are generated in parallel on Galaxy's HPC
**References** -------------- 1. Tautenhahn R, Böttcher C, Neumann S. (2008). *Highly sensitive feature detection for high resolution LC/MS.* BMC Bioinformatics, 9:504. 2. Smith CA, et al. (2006). *XCMS: processing mass spectrometry data for metabolite profiling.* Anal. Chem., 78(3):779–787.
